methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Nabla Bio is a Series A biotech company founded by Surge Biswas and Frances Anastassacos that has raised $37M in equity plus $1.5B+ in partnerships. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 8 private and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including adjacent-stage comparables such as Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, and Asimov.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($37M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression, yields $74M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private comparables produces $805M, with a peer range of $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 7.4x across three biotech tickers) cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulated range is $63M to $926M, with a median of $440M. This wide spread reflects material uncertainty: the stage-progression method grounds a conservative floor, while peer medians—driven by later-stage Series B valuations—establish an upper anchor. Key uncertainties include the contribution of the $1.5B partnership value to enterprise value, the relative stage positioning versus peers, and underlying unit economics not visible in public filings. A point estimate within $300M–$600M would appropriately weight stage proximity against peer comparables, pending deeper diligence on revenue traction and partnership terms.
